HomePage.title: Flight recorder for AI trading agents.
HomePage.lede: TradeTrace records every strategy run as a replayable audit trail: what the user asked, how Qwen parsed it, how Playbook tested it, what risks triggered, who approved it, and what happened next.
HomePage.finalFormBody: Web UI is the main demo surface. Telegram is the trigger and approval channel. Both share one run/event store.
HomePage.traceBody: Capture parse, backtest, risk, approval, and execution events in one timeline.
HomePage.governBody: Deterministic rules block martingale, high leverage, missing stop loss, and weak backtests.
HomePage.replayBody: Historical real runs can replay without live external APIs while staying auditable.

NewRunPage.title: Start the recorder.
NewRunPage.errorGeneric: Failed to create run. Check provider environment variables.
NewRunPage.nextTitle: Parse, enrich, test, score, gate, replay.
NewRunPage.nextBody: TradeTrace uses the same provider-backed path locally and online: Qwen parse, Bitget Skill Evidence Pack, Playbook backtest, Risk Ledger, approval, replay, and report.

DashboardPage.title: AgentOps for trading runs.
DashboardPage.pendingApprovals: {count, plural, =0 {No runs are waiting for human approval.} one {# run is waiting for human approval.} other {# run(s) are waiting for human approval.}}

EvidencePack.providerLine: Provider: {provider}. These skills enrich the run before risk scoring.
EvidencePack.empty: Evidence Pack will appear after Bitget skills are collected.

ReportPanel.empty: Report will appear after the run completes or is blocked.
